---
output: pdf_document
date: "2026-02-24"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
```

## Table of continents

1. EDA of the Reasoner V1 output
1. More to come?

# EDA of REsoner V1 Output

### Cleaning the data a little
```{r}
## reading in data
prompt_output_reasoner <- read.csv("PromptOutputs_RV1/PromptOuput_Reasoner_V1.csv")
prompt_output_ChatGPT <- read.csv("PromptOutputs_GPT-5-mini/PromptOutput_GPT_run_label.csv")
prompt_output_Gemini <- read.csv("Gemini_scored_answers_revised_r6.csv")
# colnames(prompt_output) the colnames are: "Index" "Question.ID" "Correct.Answer" Pushback.Level" "Response1.Answer" 
# "Response2.Answer" "Response3.Answer" "Response4.Answer" "Response5.Answer" "Response6.Answer"

### REASONER CSV CHANGES
#drop the index column because what? 
prompt_output_reasoner <- select(prompt_output_reasoner, -Column1)
# also drop the run.number column
prompt_output_reasoner <- prompt_output_reasoner %>%
  mutate(Run.Number = recode(Run.Number,
                      "run0" = 1,
                      "run1" = 2,
                      "run2" = 3)) %>%
  rename(run.Number =Run.Number)


##CHATGPT CHANGES
## adding a "Model" column to the GPT data
prompt_output_ChatGPT["Model"] <- rep("ChatGPT", nrow(prompt_output_ChatGPT))

## adding a "Subject" column ot the GPT data
# creating Question.Id <-> Subject key
Quest_sub_key <- prompt_output_reasoner %>%
  select(Question.ID, Subject) %>%
  unique()

# joining the Quest_sub_key to the ChatGPT data
prompt_output_ChatGPT2 <- merge(prompt_output_ChatGPT, Quest_sub_key, all.x=TRUE)

##GEMINI CHANGES
# adding run column (there's only one)
prompt_output_Gemini2 <- prompt_output_Gemini
prompt_output_Gemini2["run.Number"] <- rep(1, nrow(prompt_output_Gemini2))

#adding model column
prompt_output_Gemini2["Model"] <- rep("Gemini", nrow(prompt_output_Gemini2))

#adding subject via the Question.ID-Subject key
prompt_output_Gemini2 <- merge(prompt_output_Gemini2, Quest_sub_key, all.x=TRUE)

# changing column names to match ChatGPT and Reasoner
prompt_output_Gemini2 <- prompt_output_Gemini2 %>%
  rename("Response1.Answer" = "R1",
         "Response2.Answer" = "R2",
         "Response3.Answer" = "R3",
         "Response4.Answer" = "R4",
         "Response5.Answer" = "R5",
         "Response6.Answer" = "R6")

## merging the Reasoner and the ChatGPT data
prompt_outputs_R_C <- rbind(prompt_output_ChatGPT2, prompt_output_reasoner)

## merging the Reasoner-ChatGPT dataframe with Gemini
#prompt_outputs_FULL <- rbind(prompt_outputs_R_C, prompt_output_Gemini2)
  
## make the dataframe long to make analysis easier
outputs_long <- prompt_outputs_R_C %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("response"),
    names_to = "Response.Number",
    values_to = "Model.Answer"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Response.Number = str_extract(Response.Number, "\\d+") %>% as.integer()
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    correct = Model.Answer == Correct.Answer
  )

outputs_long_gemini <- prompt_output_Gemini2 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("response"),
    names_to = "Response.Number",
    values_to = "Model.Answer"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Response.Number = str_extract(Response.Number, "\\d+") %>% as.integer()) %>%
  mutate(
    correct = str_detect(Model.Answer, regex("^correct$", ignore_case = TRUE)))
    
## Sick okay NOW merging the reasoner, chat gpt, and gemini models together
outputs_long <- rbind(outputs_long, outputs_long_gemini)
```


### Accuracy by Response round
```{r}
accuracy_by_round <- outputs_long %>%
  group_by(Response.Number, Model) %>%
  summarise(
    accuracy = mean(correct, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = 6
  )

ggplot(accuracy_by_round, aes(x = Response.Number, y = accuracy, color =Model)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Accuracy by Response Round",
    x = "Response Number",
    y = "Accuracy"
  )
```


### Accuracy by Pushback level
```{r}
## creating a sub-table with the column accuracy: the average correctness grouped by pushback-level and response number
accuracy_by_pushback <- outputs_long %>%
  group_by(Pushback.Level, Response.Number, Model) %>%
  summarise(
    accuracy = mean(correct, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

accuracy_by_pushback_reasoner <- accuracy_by_pushback %>%
  filter(Model == "REASONER V1")

accuracy_by_pushback_mini <- accuracy_by_pushback  %>%
  filter(Model == "ChatGPT")

accuracy_by_pushback_gemini <- accuracy_by_pushback  %>%
  filter(Model == "Gemini")

p1 <- ggplot(accuracy_by_pushback_reasoner,
       aes(x = Response.Number,
           y = accuracy,
           color = Pushback.Level)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Accuracy by Pushback Level Reasoner V1",
    x = "Response Number",
    y = "Accuracy"
  )

p2 <- ggplot(accuracy_by_pushback_mini,
       aes(x = Response.Number,
           y = accuracy,
           color = Pushback.Level)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Accuracy by Pushback Level ChatGPT mini",
    x = "Response Number",
    y = "Accuracy"
  )

p3 <- ggplot(accuracy_by_pushback_gemini,
       aes(x = Response.Number,
           y = accuracy,
           color = Pushback.Level)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Accuracy by Pushback Level Gemini",
    x = "Response Number",
    y = "Accuracy"
  )

ggsave("accuracy_by_pushback.png", plot = p1 / p2 / p3, width = 6, height = 4, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
## creating a sub-table with the column accuracy: the average correctness grouped by pushback-level and response number
accuracy_by_subject <- outputs_long %>%
  group_by(Model, Subject, Response.Number) %>%
  summarise(
    accuracy = mean(correct, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

p_box <- ggplot(accuracy_by_subject,
       aes(x = Subject, y = accuracy, color = Subject)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.6) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.6) +
  facet_wrap(~ Model) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Accuracy by Subject and Model",
    x = "Subject",
    y = "Accuracy"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank())

ggsave("accuracy_by_subject.png", plot = p_box, width = 6, height = 4, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```


### How often does the model change it's answer?
```{r}
outputs_long <- outputs_long %>%
  group_by(Question.ID, Model) %>%
  arrange(Response.Number) %>%
  mutate(
    changed_answer = Model.Answer != lag(Model.Answer)
  )

outputs_long %>%
  summarise(
    percent_changed = mean(changed_answer, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
```


### Statistical Tests: glm and mixed effects??
```{r}
glm_model <- glm(
  correct ~ Response.Number + Pushback.Level + Model,
  data = outputs_long,
  family = binomial()
)

summary(glm_model)
```
